methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $14M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 14 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Xaira Therapeutics
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Seed), post-money $2.7B

private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Seed), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
Occam is a seed-stage biotech company founded by Ana Montero Horas and Andre Marques-Smith that has raised $14M to date. The valuation universe consists of 3 public comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers in the Biotech & Health sector, including companies such as Xaira Therapeutics, Zeta Surgical, and Ultima Genomics.

Of three valuation methods examined, only stage-progression proved applicable. A 2.5x multiplier applied to the $14M total raised proxy—standard for progression from seed to next stage—yields $35M. Peer-median analysis could not be executed due to insufficient datapoints within narrow same-stage and adjacent-stage filters (fewer than the required 3 comparables met inclusion criteria). Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples (6.1x median across the three public tickers) could not be applied because revenue data is not available for the private target.

The single-method triangulation supports a valuation range of $30M to $40M, with a median estimate of $35M. This estimate carries material uncertainty given reliance on a single method. The primary limitation is absence of revenue disclosure for Occam itself, which would enable public-comp and revenue-based private benchmarking. Founder reputation, capital efficiency relative to peers, and specific stage dynamics within the biotech subsector remain key variables not captured in this quantitative analysis.
